user_input: What must I do before tilting the hood on the Cascadia?
retrieved_contexts:
["(Freightliner Cascadia — Driver's Manual, p.16) tilt the hood open, and to return it to the operating
position. A damper controls the closing rate of the
hood and hood straps prevent the hood from over-
travel. In the operating position, the hood is secured
by a hold-down latch on each side of the hood.
To Tilt the Hood
1.
Apply the parking brakes.
2.
Release both hood hold-down latches by pulling
the ends outward.
CAUTION
Do not let the hood free-fall to the full-open posi-
tion. To do so could cause damage to the hood
or hood straps.
3.
Using the bumper step and grab handle, slowly
tilt the hood until the straps support it. See
Fig. 2.8.
To Return the Hood
1.
Grasp the grab handle, and lift the hood a just-
over-center position.
2.
As the hood goes over center, the hood damper
controls the rate of descent to the operating posi-
tion.", "(FUSO FE / FG Series — Owner's Manual, p.251) 12-8
Simple inspection and service
•
Put the gearshift lever in the “P” position.
•
Remove water or other liquids from inside the
cab before tilting. Also, remove from the cup
holders any beverage whose container is open.
•
Close both doors completely.
•
Before tilting the cab, make sure there is ade-
quate clearance in front of the cab and above it.
If there are any obstructions within the clear-
ance areas shown above, remove them.
•
Retract the radio antenna.

Breakdown assistance 339", "(FUSO FE / FG Series — Owner's Manual, p.250) with chocks.
      WARNING
• Before tilting or lowering the cab, make
sure that the area around the cab is clear
of people and obstructions. Also make
sure there is nobody in the cab.
• Never tilt the cab when the vehicle is on
any kind of slope. The cab will move too
quickly under the effect of inertia if it is
tilted on a slope, possibly causing damage
to various vehicle components. Doing so
is dangerous as the hook may not engage
completely when the cab is lowered.
• Never tilt the cab with persons inside.
• To ensure safety, two people should work
together to tilt the cab if it has a roof deck
or other heavy item attached to it. One per-
son working alone could become unable
to support the cab and have an accident
as a result.
• If any heavy item of cargo is on the roof"]
response:
Before tilting the hood on the Cascadia, you must [1]:

1. Apply the parking brakes.
2. Release both hood hold-down latches by pulling the ends outward.

**CAUTION:** Do not let the hood free-fall to the full-open position, as this could cause damage to the hood or hood straps [1].
reference: Apply the parking brakes, then release both hood hold-down latches by pulling the ends outward. Do not let the hood free-fall to the full-open position, as this could damage the hood or hood straps.

Diesel exhaust ﬂuid (DEF) freezes to slush at around

The Environmental Protection Agency’s 2010 regula-

vents clogging of the DEF metering unit injection
nozzle.']
response: The DEF tank has a 19 mm filler neck inlet to prevent the hose from a diesel outlet from being inserted [2]. This design prevents accidental contamination of the DEF tank with diesel fuel.
reference: The 19 mm filler neck inlet prevents the hose from a diesel outlet from being inserted, and the tank has a blue cap for easy identification.

The TPMS constantly monitors the pressure and

data every 3 to 5 minutes. If a tire pressure change
of 3 psi (21 kPa) or greater is detected, the sensor", "(Western Star 47X / 49X — Driver's Manual, p.32) Tire Pressure Monitoring System
The Tire Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS)
screens display tire pressure, temperature, and sen-
sor battery status. See Fig. 3.21. Maintaining correct
tire pressure increases fuel economy. Sustained high
tire temperature can cause a tire to deteriorate, lead-
ing to tread separation and blowouts.
Tire pressure can be displayed in psi or bar units.
Temperature can be displayed in °F or °C. Units of
measure are set under Settings. Sensor battery sta-
tus is displayed as a bar chart inside the outline of a
battery.
The TPMS will change the color of the tire if the tire
pressure or temperature is either too high or too low.
See Fig. 3.21. It will also change the color of the
sensor battery icon if the power levels are too low.", '(Freightliner eCascadia (electric) — Maintenance Manual, p.17) 31–01

response:
FUSO recommends using ultra low sulfur diesel fuel with a sulfur content of **15 ppm or lower** [2].

Additionally, FUSO notes that biodiesel fuel can be used if it has a 5% or lower blend ratio and complies with ASTM D-6751, which specifies fuel containing 0.0015% (basis 15 ppm) sulfur or less [1].

**Important:** Using fuel with sulfur content exceeding 15 ppm will deteriorate the performance of the emission control device and the diesel particulate filter (DPF), and your truck will not meet emission regulations [2].
reference: Fuel containing 0.0015% (15 ppm) sulfur or less is recommended; higher sulfur content degrades the DPF's performance.
